mappings.rationaleAI: African history's coverage of East African coast trade, Great Lakes kingdoms, Indian Ocean networks, and European colonial penetration provides broad context across the region's geography (A), development to c. 1856 (B), and later developments (C).

Swahili as a Bantu language with extensive Arabic loanwords reflecting Indian Ocean trade contact relates to medieval commercial contacts of Azania with Arabia and India (B.1.a) and the spread of Islam and growth of coastal towns (B.1.b).
Portuguese Indian Ocean exploration directly relates to the Portuguese invasions and occupation of East Africa from 1502 (B.1.c).
Kenya's early settlement by Cushitic and Nilotic peoples and Portuguese contact relate to Stone Age origins in the Rift Valley (B.2.a) and European exploratory activities (C.1.c).
Stone Age developments in East Africa relate to the origins of the East African interior peoples in the Rift Valley (B.2.a).
The broader slave trade system provides context for the rise of Zanzibar as the leading East African coastal power and the slave trade (C.1.b).
The British Empire's expansion into East Africa relates to European exploratory and missionary activities (C.1.c).

The post-classical period's inter-civilizational trade networks and spread of Islam relate to medieval commercial contacts with Arabia and India (B.1.a) and the Shirazi dynasty and spread of Islam (B.1.b).
The Silk Road's maritime routes connecting East Africa with broader trade networks relate to medieval commercial contacts of Azania with Arabia, India, and the Mediterranean (B.1.a).
The Nile's geography and its role in northeastern African civilizations provide context for the geography of East Africa (A).
The early modern period's European colonization relates to Portuguese invasions and occupation (B.1.c) and developments in Madagascar under European contact (B.1.d).
The Islamic Golden Age's commercial networks relate to medieval commercial contacts with Arabia (B.1.a) and the spread of Islam along the East African coast (B.1.b).
